question: Which of the following is a merit of aristocracy?

option.b: The best citizen is in control of government

solution: The best citizen is in control of government – Aristocracy is a system where governance is by the most qualified individuals.

question: The political achievement of UN is the promotion of ___
solution: The UN's primary political achievement is maintaining international peace and security.

question: The chief executive system is associated with __
solution: A chief executive system is commonly associated with a presidential system of government.

question: Nigeria's action towards the external environment is embedded in her ___
solution: A country's interaction with other nations is guided by its foreign policy. Nigeria’s foreign policy dictates its diplomatic, economic, and political relations with other countries and international organizations.

question: One major merit of the Unitary system of government is that_______?
solution: It is cheaper to operate because unitary system of government is a system of government in which power is concentrated in a single system

question: The legal right to exercise power in a state is known as______
solution: Authority is the legitimate power or right to give orders, make decisions, and enforce obedience.

question: Disenfranchisement refers to the

option.a: qualification of voters in an election
option.c: denial of the right to vote in an election
option.d: right to vote and be voted for.

solution: Disenfranchisement means being deprived of voting rights.

question: Which of the following countries had a strained relationship with Nigeria over the Angolan crisis of 1975?
solution: Nigeria opposed U.S. policy on the Angolan crisis, as Nigeria supported the MPLA while the U.S. backed UNITA.

question: The second ballot is based on the principle that a successful candidate must obtain
solution:
Before the Second Ballot System is introduce in an election is when none of the candidates wins the election by absolute majority in a general election, another election would be conducted. In this final election, only the candidates with the highest votes are allowed to contest. 

So the second ballot is based on the principle that a successful candidate must obtain Absolute Majority

Absolute Majority System is a system of voting in which a candidate must acquire more than half of the total vote cast before he can be declared winner.

question: Political authority is vested in the_________
solution:
Political Authority is the authority conferred on a persons holding political offices, e.g the president, legislators etc. and the president, legislator and others are considered to be the Government

So Political authority is vested in the Government

question: The political party serves as________

option.a: A platform upon which the rights and liberties of the people can be attained
option.b: The group aggregating the interest of all in the state
option.c: A platform for rebelling against military rule

solution:
A political party primarily serves as a platform for interest representation by:

Organizing and representing the views and interests of different groups in society.
Competing for political power through elections.
Influencing government policies in favor of their supporters.

question: A party can be said to have national outlook if________

option.a: it is ethnic based but has branches all over the state
option.b: its membership cut across ethnic boundaries
option.c: it is registered by the electoral commission as a national party
option.d: it is headed by individuals who are famous within and without the country

solution: A political party has a national outlook when it represents diverse ethnic, religious, and regional groups rather than being limited to a specific ethnic or regional interest.

question: The Brussel conference in 1889/90 was based on______

option.a: putting an end to slave trade
option.c: furthering the decision on colonialism as previously discussed in the Berlin conference
option.d: deciding on how to penetrate Africa for economic adventurism

solution: The Brussel Conference unlike the Berlin Conference was targeted at the reconsideration of the unjust virtues and vices occasioned by colonialism, thereby specifically seeking to eradicate slave trade and related activities.

question: One of the major defects of the Nigerian council which necessitated its replacement was that....

option.a: the meetings of the council were poorly attended
option.b: the council could only make law for the southern protectorate
option.c: the executive council was powerful than it

solution: The unrepresentative nature and figurehead stance of the Nigerian Council's membership is a stand-alone reason for the flaw of the platform. Most members were Whites who didn't understand the interests of the people they seek to govern.

question: The federal judge under the 1963 constitution could be removed by the______
solution: 2/3 majority support or vote of the National Assembly members was enough under the 1963 Constitution to remove a federal judge.

question: In the 4th republic, the political parties were______
solution: With a multiparty system, the three political parties in 4th Republic were the Peoples Democratic Party, All Progressive Grand Alliance and the Alliance for Democracy.

question: The most representative organ of the united nations is the_______
solution: General Assembly. The UN General Assembly comprises the Heads of State and Government of all the State Parties in the UN body.

question: Lobbying is often a strategy adopted by________
solution: Pressure groups often adopt lobbying as a negotiation mechanism with critical governement stakeholders and or legislators so that significant policies and laws giving effect to their interests are implemented.

question: Rates are generally collected in Nigeria by ________
solution: Local government generates revenue through the collection of rates on radio and television, market shops, motor parks etc

question: Which of the following is a good example of a confederal state?
solution:
In a confederal system, member states retain most of the power and delegate limited authority to the central governing body. ECOWAS is a regional organization where member countries cooperate on economic and political issues but maintain their sovereignty and control over most of their affairs. 

Switzerland and the USA are examples of federal states, where the central government has significant authority and shares power with regional governments. Nigeria is also a federal state with a similar structure to the USA and Switzerland.

question: The British Government revoked the charter of the Royal Niger Company and took over the direct administration of Nigeria in _________
solution: On 1 January 1900, the Royal Niger Company transferred its territories to the British Government. This change marked the beginning of direct British colonial rule over Nigeria, transitioning from the earlier system of indirect control through the Royal Niger Company.

question: In the traditional Hausa-Fulani political system, political authority was vested in the ___________
solution: The Caliphate was divided into emirates and each headed by an emir. The Emir was the traditional ruler and held significant political, judicial, and religious authority within the emirate. The Emirate Council, composed of various officials and advisors, assisted the Emir in governance, but the ultimate authority resided with the Emir.

question: Which of the following was the last to win independence from colonial rule ?
solution:
Angola was the last among the options to gain independence from colonial rule.

Côte d'Ivoire – Gained independence from France in 1960.
Algeria – Gained independence from France in 1962 after a long and bloody war.
Tanzania – (Formerly Tanganyika) Gained independence from Britain in 1961, later uniting with Zanzibar in 1964.
Angola – Gained independence from Portugal in 1975, making it the last among these options.

question: A meeting of the legislature is usually brought to an end with __________
solution:
A meeting of the legislature is typically brought to an end through adjournment, which means the session is temporarily suspended and will resume at a later date.

Here’s what the other options mean:

Prorogation – This is the formal ending of a parliamentary session by the head of state (e.g., the president or monarch), after which a new session will begin.
Dissolution – This permanently ends the current parliament, usually leading to new elections.
Suspension – This refers to a temporary stoppage of legislative activities, often due to disciplinary actions or special circumstances.

question: Professor lbrahim Gambari was the Special Assistant to the United Nations Secretary General on____________
solution: Professor Ibrahim Gambari was appointed as the Special Adviser to the United Nations Secretary-General on Africa in 1999. In this role, he focused on various issues pertaining to the African continent, including political, economic and social matters as well as promoting peace and development in Africa.

question: In the First Republic, the official opposition party was the ..........

option.a: National Council of Nigeria and Cameroons

solution: The official opposition party in the First Republic was the Action Group.

question: The type of government operated in Nigeria between October 1st 1979 to December 3 1983 is called a __________
solution: During this period, Nigeria operated under the Second Republic, which was modelled after the United States' presidential system. In this system, the President is both the head of state and the head of government, separate from the legislature. This period marked a shift from the parliamentary system used during the First Republic.

question: The highest body which performed legislative functions under Babangida Regime was
solution:
The Supreme Military Council, SMC was the top highest organ of the Federal Government of Nigeria in the military regimes of Johnson Aguiyi-Ironsi, General Yakubu Gowon, Murtala Mohammed, General Obasanjo and Major General Muhammadu Buhari.
The body was instituted in 1966 by the administration of Aguiyi Ironsi deriving power from statute 8 of decree 1, it was later replaced as the highest body in a military regime by President Ibrahim Babangida in 1985 who created the Armed Forces Ruling Council.

question: Nigeria is a major financier of which of the following?
solution: Nigeria is a major financier of the African Development Bank (AFDB), which is the leading financial institution in Africa, providing funding for development projects across the continent. Nigeria is one of the largest shareholders and a major contributor to the bank’s resources.

question: A constıtution that requires specific statutory procedures for amendment is said to be
solution: A rigid constitution requires specific and often more complex procedures for amendment, making it more difficult to change.s

question: The conference convened to discuss the provisions of the Macpherson Constitution was held in?
solution: On the 9th of January, 1950, delegates from the northern, eastern and western regions of Nigeria met as a body in Ibadan to discuss issues on the new constitution Sir Macpherson was drafting (Macpherson constitution of 1951).

question: In a federal system of government, legislative items constitutionally reserved for only the central government fall under the _________ list.
solution:
Exclusive Legislative List

These are the areas the powers reserved exclusively for the central government are defined. This means that only the central or federal government can legislate on the subjects in the exclusive list. Interference of the regional or state governments on matters in the exclusive list can be declared null and void and unconstitutional.

question: In a parliamentary system of government, the head of government is typically ___________.

option.b: the leader of the majority party in the legislature
option.d: chosen by the head of state

solution: In a parliamentary system of government, the head of government is typically the leader of the majority party in the legislature. This is because the head of government, often called the prime minister, is usually the leader of the political party that has the most seats in the legislature.

question: What is the main source of funding for public corporations in Nigeria?
solution: The main source of funding for public corporations in Nigeria is Government budget allocations. Public corporations are owned and operated by the government, and their operations are funded through the government's budget. This is different from private corporations, which are funded through private investments and revenues generated from their operations.

question: n which system of government is the executive and legislature fused?
solution: All members of the executive are part of the legislature.

question: In a communist system, who owns and controls the means of production?
solution: In a communist system, the means of production are owned and controlled by the government. This is because communism advocates for a classless society where wealth and power are distributed equally among the citizens. Therefore, the government controls the means of production to ensure this equal distribution.

question: Nigeria shares its longest border with which West African country?
solution: Nigeria shares its longest border with Niger. The border between Nigeria and Niger is approximately 1,497 kilometers long, making it Nigeria's longest international border.

question: The process of selecting candidates to represent a political party in elections is known as
solution: The process of selecting candidates to represent a political party in elections is known as primaries. In the primary election, party members or delegates vote for their preferred candidate from a list of candidates. The winner of the primary election becomes the party's candidate in the general election.

question: The Ijaw people of pre-colonial Nigeria were organized into?
solution: The Ijaw people of pre-colonial Nigeria were organized into clans. A clan is a group of people united by actual or perceived kinship and descent. In the case of the Ijaw people, these clans were often made up of several villages and communities that shared common ancestry.

question: NEPAD stands for

option.a: New Economic Programmes for African Development

solution:
NEPAD stands for New Partnerships for Africa's Development. It is a home-grown African initiative to overcome the age-long obstacles to Africa's development. The body partners with a group of 8 industrialised countries:

1. The Russian Federation

2. Germany

3. France

4 Italy

5. Japan

6. United Kingdom

7. Northern Ireland

8. United States of America

question: An agent in which the state undertakes direct political socialisation is the
solution: The state undertakes direct political socialisation through the schools. This is done through the Ministry of Education that provides curriculums that contain subjects such as Civic Education, Government, Security Education, etc to ensure that pupils/ students become aware of the political situation and understand the values, beliefs and attitudes shared by members of the society.

question: The judgement that ceded and transferred Bakassi legal title from Nigeria to Cameroon was delivered by

option.b: Court of Justice of the African Union

solution: The judgement that ceded and transferred the Bakassi Peninsula's legal title from Nigeria to Camerron was delivered by the International Court of Justice (ICJ). The case, titled Land and Maritime Boundary between Cameroon and Nigeria, resolved the long-standing boundary dispute between the two countries.

question: ECOWAS was established in
solution: The Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) was established on the 28th of May, 1975 under the regime of General Yakubu Gowon. It has its administrative headquarters in Abuja, Nigeria and its financial headquarters in Lome, Togo.

question: The Nigerian Civil War was fought during the military regime of
solution: The Nigerian Civil War took place between 1967-70 under the regime of General Yakubu Gowon (1966-75). It was a war between Eastern Nigeria (Biafra) and Nigeria.

question: Lack of separation of power is a feature of
solution: Separation of power is the division of powers and functions among the three arms of government - the legislature, executive and judiciary. This is essential to prevent the abuse of power when it is concentrated in the hands of a single authority. In a presidential system, there is a clear separation of power. In a cabinet system, there is still separation of power although, it is not as clear (this is because the legislature and executive arms are fused). However, in a military government, power is concentrated in the hands of the military leader. He has the power to make laws through the promulgation of decrees; to execute the law; as well as to punish law breakers. In this case, there is a lack of separation of power.

question: An economic system that encourages state ownership and control of the means of production is
solution: Socialism is an economic system that encourages state ownership and control of the means of production. Communism, on the other hand, is the common ownership and control of the means of production.

question: Which local government reform in Nigeria prevented traditional rulers from engaging in politics?
solution: The 1976 Reform of the Local Government in Nigeria changed the multiple system of local government councils to a single-tier all-purpose local government. It also led to the exclusion of traditional rulers from local government councils.

question: Price stability in the international oil market is the major objective of the
solution: The major objective of the Organisation of Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) is to ensure price stability in the international oil market. This is to ensure that fluctuation is eliminated and that every member-state can benefit from oil sales.

question: A constitution that requires a plebiscite or a referendum to be amended is
solution:
Plebiscite; the direct vote of all the members of an electorate on an important public question such as a change in the constitution.

A referendum; is a direct and universal vote in which an entire electorate is invited to vote on a particular proposal and can have nationwide or local forms.

A rigid Constitution;  is a constitution that is difficult or slow to change usually because of a prescribed process of amendment that is detailed and lengthy in execution.

question: The major factor that influenced the formulation of Nigerian foreign policy in the First Republic was
solution:
The major factor that influenced the formulation of Nigerian foreign policy in the First Republic was the colonial legacy.

According to J.U. Anyaele (1994), we can rightly agree that the colonial legacy fore-ordained our foreign policy initially. This is one of the major reasons why the Balewa Administration maintains close relations mainly with Britain and other commonwealth countries.

question: Nigeria's recognition of the Popular Movement for the Liberation of Angola to lead that country was made under
solution: Under General Murtala Mohammed's regime, Nigeria adopted an assertive foreign policy supporting liberation movements. During his short tenure in 1975–1976, Nigeria recognized the Popular Movement for the Liberation of Angola (MPLA) as the legitimate leadership of Angola when it gained independence.

question: The first Nigerian Permanent Representative to the United Nations was
solution:
Among the four listed options, Chief Simeon Adebo served earliest as Nigeria’s Permanent Representative to the United Nations. He held this position from 1962 to 1967, which is before the tenures of Alhaji Yusuf Maitama Sule (1979–1983), General Joseph Garba (1984–1989), and Professor Ibrahim Gambari (1990–1999).

Although Jaja Wachuku (1960–1961) and Muhammad Ngileruma (1961–1962) served before Adebo, they are not among the answer choices, making Chief Adebo the first among the listed options.

question: Nigeria's departure from pro-west policy during the Murtala Muhammed regime was as a result of

option.a: the economic interest of the West in Nigeria
option.c: the growing trade between Nigeria and the East
option.d: Nigeria's concern for decolonization in Africa

solution:
Nigeria departure from pro-west policy during the Murata Mohammed regime was a result of Nigeria's concern for decolonization in Africa.

Among the regime's achievements was its spectacular support for decolonization and self-determination as epitomized by its recognition of Agostino Neto's Popular Movement for the Liberation of Angola (MPLA)

question: Citizenship in a modern state expresses the status of a person who possesses
solution:
Citizenship in a modern state expresses the status of a person who possesses full political rights.

This is so as a person who is a legal member of a state is citizen of that state. He must also owe allegiance to that particular state he resides and then enjoys full civil and political rights and will be ready to put his life at stake in defence of that country whenever occasion calls for that

question: Communism is a system which recognizes
solution:
Communism is a system which recognizes the existence of the state.

This is so as communism may be defined as an economic system and a system of government where the means of production, distribution and exchange are totally controlled by the state.

Some features of communism are as follows:

1. Total ownership of means of production and distribution, by the state.

2. Absence of classes, ie classes society.

3. Centrally planned economy.

4. Authoritarian and Totalitarian government

question: The absence of the rule of law in government will bring about
solution:
The absence of the rule of law in government will bring about human rights abuse.

This is so as the rule of law refers to the constitutional provision which emphasizes the supremacy of the law, the equality before the law and existence of fundamental human rights.

question: Centralization of power is the basic feature of
solution:
Centralization of power is the basic feature of a Unitary government.

Centralization is the process by which government power is located in a single central authority. The highest degree of centralization is realized where all powers of government are vested in a central authority. The degree of centralization is highest in a unitary state.

question: A major way of maintaining confidence in the electoral process is by ensuring that

option.b: elections are conducted in a free and fair atmosphere
option.d: elections are conducted as and when due

solution: Maintaining confidence in the electoral process primarily depends on ensuring that elections are free, fair, and transparent. This means that all participants have equal opportunities to participate, vote, and be represented, thereby fostering trust in the outcomes and integrity of the electoral system.

question: The fees collected by local governments at motor parks represents
solution: The fees collected by local governments at motor parks represents levies

question: In pre-colonial Igbo society, decisions on public issues were reached mainly through
solution:
In Pre-colonial Igbo society, decisions on public issues were reached mainly through consensus.

The decision were then taken back to the respective families for ratification or rejection.

question: Nigeria formally became a federation in
solution:
Nigeria formally became a federation 1946.

It should be noted that the 1946 constitution only laid foundation for federalism. The constitution charged the provinces to three regions of North, East West regional assemblies without legislative powers.

In 1951, the Macpherson Constitution gave legislative powers on specific issues to the regional governments.

The true federalism was then established in 1954 by the Oliver Lyttleton Constitution with the division lists between the Central and the Regional governments.

question: The United Nations charter is an instrument that

option.a: sets out the rights and obligations of member states
option.b: provides for funding of memeber states
option.c: facilitates the resolution of disputes among member states
option.d: determines the admission of member states

solution:
The United Nations charter is an instrument that sets out the rights and obligations of member states.

It should be recalled that one of the aims and objectives of U.N.O is to promote respect for Fundamental Human Rights among Nations.

question: The impact of the Commonwealth of Nations is felt most in the area of
solution:
The impact of the Commonwealth of Nations is felt most in the area of economic cooperation.

Nigeria suffered much hardship by the time she was suspended by the Union in 1997 as she was accused of violating the Fundamental Human Rights of Individuals.

question: Professor Ibrahim Gambari is the Special Assistant to the United Nations Secretary General on
solution: Professor Ibrahim Gambari served as the Under-Secretary-General and Special Adviser on Africa to the United Nations Secretary-General.

question: A common feature of government is
solution:
A common feature of government is the making of public policy.

Government is made up of three arms the legislature, the executive and judiciary. The main function of the legislature is law making while that of the executive is formulation and implementation of policies

question: Associations whose main interest is to influence public policies without having to capture power are
solution:
Associations whose main interest is to influence public policy without having to capture power are pressure groups.

Pressure groups are defined as association of people who seek to influence government actions and policies to the benefit of their members.

question: Constitutional disputes in states with written constitutions are resolved by
solution:
Constitutional disputes in a state with written constitution are resolved by the Judiciary.

One of the main functions of the judiciary is to interpret the laws of the land in courts.

It settles disputes between the executive and the legislature; between individuals and the state, etc.

The judiciary interprets and defends the constitution. It has also full power to declare any action unconstitutional if such action goes against the dictates of the constitution especially in a state with written constitution.

question: Public opinion becomes politically relevant when it
solution: Public opinion becomes politically relevant when it actively shapes and influences the decisions made by the government. While public opinion can aggregate views, criticize those in power, or show support, its true political relevance is demonstrated when it translates into pressure on policymakers and affects policy outcomes. This influence on government decisions is key to the functioning of a democratic system, as it holds leaders accountable and ensures that public concerns are reflected in governance.

question: The Eastern and Western regions of Nigeria achieved the status of self-government is
solution:
The Eastern and Western regions of Nigeria achieved the status of self-government in 1957.

Western Region: Attained self-government on August 8, 1957, under the leadership of Chief Obafemi Awolowo.

Eastern Region: Followed shortly after, gaining self-government on October 1, 1957, led by Dr. Nnamdi Azikiwe.

This milestone was part of Nigeria's gradual decolonization process under British rule, culminating in the country's full independence on October 1, 1960. The Northern Region achieved self-government later, in 1959, due to its more cautious approach to political reforms.

question: The Bureau for Public Enterprises is charged with the responsibility for
solution: The Bureau for Public Enterprises is charged with the responsibility for privatization and commercialization

question: In the First Republic,politics in the Northern region was dominated by the
solution:
In the First Republic,politics in the Northern region was dominated by the NPC.
The Northern People's Congress (NPC) was founded in October 1951. It sprang up from the northern cultural organization known as Jamiyy or Mutanen Arewa which was founded in 1949. NPC was highly dominated by the Hausa Fulani. Throughout its existence, it controlled the northern regional government. In the centre, it some times formed coalition government with NCNC.

UMBC and NEPU were some of its oppsing political parties in the northern regional house of assembly.

question: The dispute between Nigeria and Cameroon is over
solution:
The dispute between Nigeria and Cameroon is over territory.

The people of Cameroun have been calming that Bakasi pennisula belongs to them. But Nigeria strongly maintains that the pennisula is one of her territories.

question: Nigeria's influence in OPEC is determined by the

option.d: low sulphur content of her crude

solution:
Nigeria's influence in OPEC is determined by volume of oil revenue.

Nigeria has its own trust fund which is estimated at about ninety million naria in the African Development Bank (ADB). From this aid is given to needy countries. This marvelous achievement is due to the volume of Nigeria's oil revenues. This in no small measure boosts the economy of Nigeria.

question: The non-British colony which is a member of the Common wealth is
solution: Mozambique was never a British colony; it was a Portuguese colony. However, it joined the Commonwealth of Nations in 1995, becoming the first country with no historical ties to Britain to do so. Other non-British colonies that later joined the Commonwealth include Rwanda, which became a member in 2009. However, since this question was set in 2003, Mozambique is the best answer to this question.

question: The major problem of the ECOWAS is lack of
solution: ECOWAS has long aspired to deepen economic integration among its member states by adopting a single currency. However, the significant economic disparities, divergent monetary policies, and the challenges of political coordination have hindered the implementation of a common currency. This lack remains a major obstacle to achieving a more unified economic space in West Africa.

question: The independent African countries that signed the OAU Charter on May 25th 1963 were
solution:
The Organisation of African Unity (OAU) Charter was signed on 25 May 1963 by 30 independent African nations during the Addis Ababa conference. Notably, Morocco and Togo were not among the original signatories on that date:

Morocco signed the charter later, on 19 September 1963.

Togo also signed later, with its ratification recorded in 1963 but not on the founding date.

Key Details from the Signatories:

Original Signatories (25 May 1963):
Algeria, Burundi, Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad, Congo (Brazzaville), Dahomey (Benin), Ethiopia, Gabon, Ghana, Guinea, Ivory Coast, Liberia, Libya, Madagascar, Mali, Mauritania, Niger, Nigeria, Rwanda, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Somalia, Sudan, Tanganyika (Tanzania), Tunisia, Uganda, United Arab Republic (Egypt), Upper Volta (Burkina Faso), and others

question: The responsibility for admitting new members to the OPEC rests with the
solution: The authority to admit new members to OPEC resides with the OPEC Conference, which is the organization's supreme decision-making body. Composed of delegations from all member countries, the Conference meets biannually and requires a three-quarters majority vote (including approval from all five founding members) to admit a new state.

question: A common feature of the earlier political parties in Nigeria was that they
solution:
A common feature of the earlier political parties in Nigeria was that they started as a social-cultural organizations.

For instance the defunct action Group originated from Yoruba cultural organisation known as Egbe Omo Oduduwa.

question: Judicial administrations in respect of National Code of Conduct lies with the
solution: The Judicial administrations in respect of National Code of Conduct lies with the Code of Conduct Bureau

question: The process of nationalism was accelerated by

option.a: the signing of the Atlantic Charter

solution: The Atlantic Charter (signed in 1941 by Winston Churchill and Franklin D. Roosevelt) accelerated nationalism in colonized territories by explicitly endorsing the principle of self-determination. After World War II, nationalist leaders in Africa and Asia (e.g., Kwame Nkrumah, Jomo Kenyatta) cited the Charter to demand independence, arguing that colonial rule violated the Allies' proclaimed commitment to freedom and equality.

question: Laws made by state governments are known as
solution: Laws made by state governments are known as edicts. It should be noted that edicts are the laws made by the state military governments.

question: In a democracy,sovereign authority is exercised by the
solution: In a democracy, sovereign authority is exercised by the electorate

question: A distinctive feature of the Richards Constitution was its provision for

option.a: unofficial members in the Legislative Council
option.b: a Legislative Council for the whole of Nigeria
option.d: official members in the Legislative Council

solution:
A distinctive feature of the Richards Constitution was its provision for a Legislative Council for the whole of Nigeria.

Northern and Southern Niger were brought together under one Legislative Council. That is, the same laws were made for the whole country by the same legislature.

question: In the First Republic,the official opposition party was the

option.d: National Council of Nigeria and the Cameroons

solution: The official opposition party in the First Republic was the Action Group.

question: The principle of checks and balances empowers the judiciary to

option.a: invalidate the actions of the other arms

solution:
The principle of checks and balances empowers the judiciary to invalidate the actions of the other arms.
For instance, the judges who can try government officials can as well declare a legislation ultra vires.

question: The method used to determine the possible outcome of an electoral contest is
solution:
The method used to determine the possible outcome of an electoral contest is opinion poll.

Opinion poll is the questioning of a number of people chosen by chance to find out general opinion about something or someone. In civilized societies, newspaper columns contain public opinions on government activities.

question: An unwritten Constitution is

option.c: partially written and based on convention
option.d: completely unwritten and based on convention

solution:
An unwritten constitution is partially written and based on conventions.

F. Adigwe (1974) defined unwritten constitution as that made up of several enactments and conventions like the British constitution. He also pointed out that an attempt was made to codify some of, but not all, the rules and principles, during the regime of Cromwell.

question: A member of the Commonwealth that was not colonized by Britain is
solution: Nearly all members of the Commonwealth of Nations were at one time under British Jurisdiction, by realm, colony, dominion or protectorate. Two nations however are member states, that do not fall into this category, Mozambique and Rwanda. Mozambique and Rwanda were nations that were under French control before their independence.

question: A key characteristics of the civil service is
solution: A fundamental characteristic of a professional civil service is that it is based on a merit system. This means that appointments, promotions, and other personnel decisions are made based on qualifications, competence, and performance rather than on political connections, favoritism, or quotas. This merit-based approach helps ensure that the civil service is efficient, impartial, and able to deliver quality public services.

question: The right that enables citizens to express their electoral preferences is
solution:
Franchise the right that enables citizens to express their electoral preferences.

It should be noted that there are two types of franchise which are as follows:

1. Restricted Franchise or Limited Franchise

2. Unlimited Franchise or Universal Adult Suffrage.

question: One merit of a written constitution is its
solution:
A written constitution's ease of reference is a key merit. Being codified into a single document or organized set of texts allows for:

Clarity and Accessibility: Citizens, lawmakers, and judges can easily consult it to understand governmental structures, rights, and legal limits.

Transparency: Provides a definitive source of law, reducing ambiguity and arbitrary governance.

Judicial Reliance: Courts use it as a primary reference for resolving disputes and upholding constitutional principles.

question: Which of the following justifies the Afro-centric foreign policy of Nigeria?

option.a: Her location in the West African sub-region
option.b: Her colonization by a foriegn power
option.c: Her physical size, population and resource endowments
option.d: Her ability to contribute to peace keeping missions

solution:
Her ability to contribute to peace keeping missions justifies the Afro-centric foreign policy of Nigeria.

was highly committed to peaceful settlement of political crises and inter state disputes.

She played very prominent role in the settlement of political crises in Liberia and Sierra Leone through the formation of ECOMOG.

question: The officer in charge of the administration of the palace in the Yoruba political structure is the
solution: In the Yoruba political structure, particularly within the Oyo Empire, the Ilari were palace officials appointed by the Alaafin (king) to manage administrative duties within the palace. They acted as emissaries, enforcers of royal decrees, and overseers of palace operations, ensuring the smooth functioning of daily activities and ceremonies.

question: Which of the following in the Sokoto Caliphate performed functions similar to that of the Bashorun in Oyo Kingdom
solution:
Waziri in the Sokoto Caliphate performed functions similar to that of the Bashorum in Oyo Kingdom.

In the Sokoto Caliphate , Waziri is the senior official, the Emir's administrative adviser who acted as the Prime Minister. In Oyo Kingdom, the Bashorum was the Prime Minister of the empire

question: The fear of domination in the public service led to the

option.a: adoption of population as a formula for revenue allocation
option.b: establishment of the Civil Service Commission
option.d: establishment of the Federal Character Commission

solution: In Nigeria, there was a significant concern that one group or region might dominate the public service, leading to imbalances in representation and access to government appointments. To counteract this, the government established the Federal Character Commission. This commission was created to ensure that public offices are distributed equitably among the various ethnic, regional, and social groups in the country. Its purpose is to promote fairness and prevent any single group from dominating the public service.

question: The immediate cause of the 1966 military coup de'etat in Nigeria was
solution: The immediate cause of the 1966 military coup de'etat in Nigeria was political instability. It should be noted that the Action Group Crises of 1962 was one of the crisis that led to the military take-over of government in January 1966. The 1962 Action Group crisis continued to rear its ugly head, until Western Nigeria election crisis of 1965. The disagreement over the election result between UPGA and NNA in Western region resulted to a crisis which led to a breakdown of law and order. This situation attracted military intervention on 15th January, 1966.

question: The power to nominate members of the judiciary at the federal level under the 1999 Constitution is vested in the
solution: Under Nigeria’s 1999 Constitution, the National Judicial Council (NJC) is vested with the authority to nominate members of the federal judiciary for appointment by the President. This process ensures judicial independence and adherence to constitutional standards.

question: Prior to 1976, the local government system practiced in Northern Nigeria was
solution: Prior to 1976, the Local government system practised in Northern Nigerian Nigeria was Chief-in-Council.By 1952, the Northern region abolished the sole Native Authority and put in its place, Chief-in-Council and Chief-and-Council and. The democratization process in the Northern region was further aided by the Native Authority Law of 1954 which was amended by the Northern Nigeria Law of 1963. They provided for five Native Authorities including Chief-in-Council and the Federal Native Authorities.

question: An advantage of flexible constitution is that it allows for
solution: An advantage of flexible constitution is that it allows for ease of amendment. Changes can be made easily in order to accommodate changing situations in the society. Its amendment does not require or involve much time and financial cost.

question: A prominent feature of a presidential system of government is
solution: In a presidential system of government, the three branches: executive, legislative, and judicial, are distinctly separate and operate independently of each other. This separation of powers is a hallmark of the presidential model, ensuring that no single branch becomes too dominant and that each branch can check and balance the others. This contrasts with parliamentary systems, where the executive is typically derived from the legislature and can be removed through a vote of no confidence.

question: A state with a hegemonic political party is one in which
solution: A State with a hegemonic political party is one in which there is only one dominant party. In this system, one party is firmly established in office. The party has hegemony, that is, it enjoys unchallenged authority. The law does not operate formally against any opposition, but clearly the state concerned is effectively single-party for longer period of many years. For example, South Africa was an example of a hegemonic party. There, the National party came into power in 1948, and it only left when Dr. Nelson Mandela was elected president in 1994.

question: The first Governor-General of Nigeria was
solution: Fredrick Lord Lugard 1914-1919

question: Federalism was introduced in Nigeria under the
solution:
The Lyttleton Constitution was enacted in 1954 and it established the federal system of government and paved the way for independence.

This constitution is considered the start of federalism, as it provided for a division of power between the central government and the regions, unlike the Richards constitution that created provinces/regions, with no autonomy being given to the regions.

question: The dominant idea behind the establishment of the Organization of African Unity is

option.b: to show the world that Africa can also do what Latin American and Asian countries have done
option.c: to provide a framework and opportunities for co-operation on common African problems
option.d: to promote economic development of Africa

solution: It is a forum to unite Africa and eradicate colonialism and imperialism.

question: The Independent National Electoral Commission has the power to prepare and maintain the register of
solution: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) of Nigeria is constitutionally mandated to conduct the registration of eligible voters and to prepare, maintain, and revise the register of voters. This function ensures that all qualified citizens are accurately documented and able to participate in elections.

question: The two leaders that played the most prominent roles in the formation of the ECOWAS were
solution: Both Leaders (Gen. Yakubu Gowon of Nigeria and Eyadema of Togo) used their military might to facilitate the organization.

question: Which of these constitutions recognised local government as a third tier of government in Nigeria?
solution: The 1979 Constitution of Nigeria recognised local government as the third tier of government. This was a significant development in Nigeria's constitutional history, as it formalised the structure and functions of local governments, granting them autonomy and recognition alongside the federal and state governments. The earlier constitutions, such as the 1960 and 1963 constitutions, did not provide for local governments as a distinct tier, and the 1946 Constitution primarily focused on colonial administration without recognizing local governments in this way.
